title: Preparation of Denaturing Agarose Gel for RNA Analysis
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"78sm7","text":"Note: RNA is subject to rapid degradation by RNase present in the environment. For optimal results, use water treated with DEPC.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Melt 1.0 g agarose in 87 ml of DEPC water, by dispersing the agarose uniformly and heating in a microwave until all particles are dissolved.
Add 10 ml 10X MOPS Buffer and 3 ml 37% formaldehyde. Use DEPC treated water and RNase free reagents. 10X MOPS Buffer by Irina Agarkova
Pour standard agarose gel gel. USE A FUME HOOD!
FORMALDEHYDE IS VOLATILE AND TOXIC. WORK IN A HOOD FROM THIS POINT FORWARD.
Allow gel to set for 1 hour. 01:00:00
Run gel in 1X MOPS Buffer. 10X MOPS Buffer by Irina Agarkova
Mix 40 µl sample buffer with 10 µl sample, heat to 55 °C 15 minutes. 00:15:00 The sample buffer is comprised of 65% formamide, 22% formalin (37% formaldehyde) and 13% 10X MOPS.
Add 10µl of the following: 50% glycerol 1 mM EDTA 0.3% each bromophenol blue and 0.3% xylene cyanol.
creator: Irina Agarkova
